summary:
The most common type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma in the orbit is B-cell lymphoma, specifically the MALT subtype, which accounts for approximately 90% of cases due to its strong association with inflammatory conditions near eye tissue.
```
```markdown
The most prevalent form of non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma found in the orbits is B-cell lymphoma, predominantly the MALT subtype, comprising about 90% of such tumors given its correlation with local inflammatory processes characteristic of orbital environments.
```
